2603.24472.title: Why Does Self-Distillation (Sometimes) Degrade the Reasoning Capability of LLMs?
2603.24472.paper_summary: Self-distillation has emerged as an effective post-training paradigm for LLMs, often improving performance while shortening reasoning traces. However, in mathematical reasoning, we find that it can reduce response length while degrading performance. We trace this degradation to the suppression of epistemic verbalization - the model's expression of uncertainty during reasoning. Through controlled experiments varying conditioning context richness and task coverage, we show that conditioning the teacher on rich information suppresses uncertainty expression, enabling rapid in-domain optimization with limited task coverage but harming OOD performance, where unseen problems benefit from expressing uncertainty and adjusting accordingly. Across Qwen3-8B, DeepSeek-Distill-Qwen-7B, and Olmo3-7B-Instruct, we observe performance drops of up to 40%. Our findings highlight that exposing appropriate levels of uncertainty is crucial for robust reasoning and underscore the importance of optimizing reasoning behavior beyond merely reinforcing correct answer traces.
2603.24472.paper_summary_zh: 自我蒸餾已成為大型語言模型（LLMs）的一種有效後訓練範式，通常能改善性能，同時縮短推理痕跡。<lb/>然而，在數學推理中，我們發現它可能會在降低響應長度的同時，損害性能。<lb/>我們將這種劣化追溯到認知語言化的抑制——模型在推理過程中表達不確定性的能力。<lb/>通過控制實驗，變化條件上下文的豐富性和任務覆蓋範圍，我們顯示對教師進行豐富信息的條件設定會抑制不確定性表達，使得在有限的任務覆蓋下能夠快速進行領域內優化，但卻損害了超出分佈（OOD）性能，因為在未見問題中，表達不確定性並相應調整是有益的。<lb/>在 Qwen3-8B、DeepSeek-Distill-Qwen-7B 和 Olmo3-7B-Instruct 中，我們觀察到性能下降高達 40%。<lb/>我們的研究結果強調，適當程度的不確定性表達對於穩健的推理至關重要，並突顯了優化推理行為的重要性，超越僅僅強化正確答案痕跡的範疇。
2603.24472.author: Jeonghye Kim et.al.
2603.24472.authors: Jeonghye Kim, Xufang Luo, Minbeom Kim, Sangmook Lee, Dohyung Kim, Jiwon Jeon, Dongsheng Li, Yuqing Yang
